visual_checks: When tabbing through the page, check if any focused element is fully hidden behind a sticky header, fixed footer, floating banner, cookie consent bar, or chat widget
Look for sticky or fixed-position elements (navigation bars, toolbars, banners, floating action buttons) that could cover focused elements as the user scrolls and tabs
Check if modal overlays, non-modal dialogs, or toast notifications obscure the currently focused element
Verify that when focus moves to an element near the top or bottom of the viewport, it is not fully hidden behind a sticky header or fixed footer
Look for cookie consent banners, promotional overlays, or chat widgets that remain on screen and may cover focused content underneath
pass_conditions: No focused element is entirely hidden by author-created content — at least part of the focus indicator is always visible
Sticky headers and fixed footers do not completely cover focused elements when the user Tabs to them
Cookie consent banners, chat widgets, and floating action buttons are positioned so they do not fully obscure focused elements
When a non-modal dialog or toast notification appears, it does not completely cover the element that currently has focus
The page scrolls or adjusts to ensure focused elements are at least partially visible when sticky or fixed elements are present
fail_conditions: A focused element is entirely hidden behind a sticky header or fixed navigation bar, with no part of the element or its focus indicator visible
A cookie consent banner or promotional overlay covers the full area of a focused element, making it invisible to the user
A fixed-position chat widget completely obscures a focused link or button in the lower-right corner of the page
A toast notification appears directly over the currently focused element, hiding it entirely
Sticky footer or bottom navigation bar covers focused elements near the bottom of the page with no visible portion remaining
na_conditions: Page has no fixed-position, sticky, or floating elements that could obscure content
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Cannot test focus obscuring from static screenshots — keyboard navigation through the page with fixed elements is required
Sticky or fixed elements are dynamically loaded and not present in the initial capture
common_mistakes: Sticky header that is tall enough to cover focused elements near the top of the scrolled viewport when the browser scrolls an element into view
Cookie consent banner that sits at the bottom of the viewport and covers links or buttons in the footer when the user Tabs to them
Chat widget floating button that overlaps the last few focusable elements in the page flow
Scroll-into-view behavior (scrollIntoView or browser default) that scrolls a focused element to be just barely visible but behind a sticky header
Fixed promotional banner that covers focused elements and has no dismiss button or close mechanism
Non-modal notifications (toasts) that appear directly over the focused element and do not reposition
auditor_anti_patterns: DO NOT confuse 'focused element is below the fold / requires scrolling' with 'focused element is obscured by a sticky overlay'. An element that the browser will auto-scroll into view when focused is NOT a 2.4.11 failure -- it is normal keyboard navigation. The SC fails ONLY when, after the focus-induced scroll completes, a sticky/fixed/floating AUTHOR-created overlay still ENTIRELY covers the focused element.

DO NOT flag focused elements that appear partially covered. SC 2.4.11 (Minimum) explicitly allows partial occlusion -- that is the stricter SC 2.4.12 territory. The verdict is 'failure' only when the element is ENTIRELY hidden.

DO NOT infer a 2.4.11 failure from screenshots that do not include a focused state. The full-page screenshot has no focus on it. Use the deterministic tab walk's per-stop focus screenshots, which capture the post-focus-scroll state with overlays in their actual position.

DO NOT report 'obscured by sticky element' if the page has no sticky/fixed/floating author content (na_conditions). Verify the page actually has a sticky header / fixed footer / floating chat / cookie banner before claiming this SC fails.
DO NOT confuse browser UI (address bar, find bar, scrollbar) with author-created overlays. User-agent UI is explicitly out of scope.

examples.pass: The page has a sticky header (80px tall) and uses scroll-padding-top: 100px in CSS, so when the browser scrolls a focused element into view it clears the sticky header, and the cookie consent banner at the bottom has enough margin that footer links remain visible when focused
examples.fail: A sticky navigation bar covers 120px at the top of the viewport, and when a user Tabs to an anchor link target near the top of a section, the focused element scrolls behind the sticky header and is completely hidden — the user cannot see what has focus
examples.partial: Most focused elements are visible, but a persistent chat widget in the lower-right corner completely covers the 'Back to top' button when it receives focus, and one footer link is fully hidden behind the cookie consent banner

plain_meaning: When a user tabs to a component and it receives keyboard focus, that component must not be ENTIRELY hidden by author-created content such as sticky headers, sticky footers, cookie banners, or floating chat widgets. At least some part of the focused control must remain visible. User-agent-created UI such as the browser find bar is excluded.
scope_applies_to: Focused interactive components in pages with sticky or fixed overlays
Sticky headers, sticky footers, floating chat bubbles, cookie banners, and similar author-created overlays
Any author overlay that can cover a focused element entirely
Scroll positions where a newly focused element lands underneath an overlay
scope_does_not_apply_to: User-agent UI such as the browser address bar or find bar
Cases where only part of the component is covered (that is the stricter SC 2.4.12)
Whether the focus indicator itself is visible on a visible element (that is SC 2.4.7)
Pages with no sticky or overlay content at all
borderline_calibration: Only flag when evidence shows the focused component is ENTIRELY hidden, not merely partially covered.
Partial occlusion is acceptable under 2.4.11 (though not under 2.4.12).
If there is no sticky or floating author content on the page, this SC is not applicable.
Rely on captured tab walk screenshots rather than guessing about overlay behavior.
